summary: Fair‑quality recording of a solid Alpine show, highlighted by a Shakedown sandwich and a powerful Space → Other One → Wharf Rat sequence.
review: The performance is remembered for its energetic first set (Half Step → Franklin’s → Dancin’, Samson & Delilah) and a second‑set Shakedown sandwich that many fans cite as a peak moment, especially the intense Space/Other One/Wharf Rat stretch.  The tape itself is of mixed fidelity – listeners note it isn’t as clear as some audience recordings but remains listenable.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the show positively (4‑5 stars) and praise specific musical moments, while a few criticize Jerry’s vocal stamina and note the recording isn’t pristine; overall rating reflects strong show quality with only fair audio.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No explicit source type mentioned; some listeners say the version lacks clarity compared to better audience tapes, but no major drop‑outs reported.

standout_songs: Space > The Other One > Wharf Rat
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set opens strong and closes with a classic Samson & Delilah, second set keeps the momentum with a Shakedown sandwich and an intense Space/Other One/Wharf Rat sequence.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Mixed – some note he sounded out of breath and couldn’t hold notes, while others praise his guitar work and vocal spin during the Space‑Other One‑Wharf Rat stretch.
band_member_comments.Bob: Positive mention of Bob’s (Bobby) voice being pushed to an intense level in the Space/Other One/Wharf Rat segment.
band_member_comments.Drums: Highly praised; Bill and Mickey’s communication on drums is described as heroic.
